subject: computer-science
description: Database Systems MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: A database is best described as:
options: Has data and relationships, and is self-describing
mcqs.explanation: A database has data and relationships. It is called 'self-describing' because it contains a description of itself.

mcqs.q: Why is a database called self-describing?
options: Because it contains a description of itself (data + schema)
Because it prints its own ID
mcqs.explanation: A database is called self-describing because it contains a description of itself — it stores both the data and the structure (schema) that defines that data.

mcqs.q: What does DBMS stand for?
mcqs.explanation: DBMS stands for Database Management System — a software package for creating and managing databases.

mcqs.q: A DBMS is best described as:
options: A software package for creating and managing databases
mcqs.explanation: A DBMS is a software package for creating and managing databases. It acts as an interface between the database application and the database.

mcqs.q: In an enterprise-class database system, the DBMS acts as the interface between:
options: The database application and the database
The OS and the cooling fan
mcqs.explanation: In an enterprise-class database system, the DBMS acts as the interface between the database application and the database — applications never read the raw data files directly.

mcqs.q: How many basic forms do databases typically have?
mcqs.explanation: Databases typically have one of two basic forms.

mcqs.q: A Flat File Database stores data in:
options: A plain text file with each line typically holding one record
mcqs.explanation: A Flat File Database stores data in a plain text file, with each line of text typically holding one record.

mcqs.q: A Hierarchical Database Model resembles which structure?
mcqs.explanation: The Hierarchical Database Model resembles a tree structure — similar to a folder architecture in the computer system.

mcqs.q: In an Object-Oriented Database, information is represented as:
options: Objects, with different types of relationships possible between two or more objects
mcqs.explanation: In an Object-Oriented Database, information is represented as objects, with different types of relationships possible between two or more objects.

mcqs.q: IDMS implemented which database model?
mcqs.explanation: IDMS (Integrated Database Management System) implemented the CODASYL DBTG model — a famous network database model.

mcqs.q: Prior to 1970, where was most data stored?
options: In separate files, mostly on reels of magnetic tape
mcqs.explanation: Prior to 1970, all data was stored in separate files, which were mostly stored on reels of magnetic tape.

mcqs.q: Which family of standards is very important in database processing today?
mcqs.explanation: The XML family of standards is very important in database processing today — used for data interchange and document storage.

mcqs.q: A Relational Database contains:
options: Multiple tables of data with rows and columns that relate to each other through special key fields
mcqs.explanation: A Relational Database contains multiple tables of data with rows and columns that relate to each other through special key fields.

mcqs.q: Who created the relational database model?
mcqs.explanation: The relational database model was created by E. F. Codd.

mcqs.q: In a relational database, data in different tables is connected through:
mcqs.explanation: In a relational database, tables are connected through special key fields (primary key in one table linked to a foreign key in another).

mcqs.q: How many basic types of relationships exist in a relational database?
mcqs.explanation: There are four types of relationships in a relational database: One-to-One, One-to-Many, Many-to-One and Many-to-Many.

mcqs.q: Which relationship type fits 'one person has one passport'?
mcqs.explanation: 'One person has one passport' is a One-to-One relationship — one record in one table relates to one record in another.

mcqs.q: Which relationship type fits 'one teacher teaches many students'?
mcqs.explanation: 'One teacher teaches many students' is a One-to-Many relationship — one record relates to multiple records in another table.

mcqs.q: Which relationship type fits 'many students belong to one class'?
mcqs.explanation: 'Many students belong to one class' is a Many-to-One relationship — multiple records relate to one record in another table.

mcqs.q: Which relationship type fits 'many students enrolled in many courses'?
mcqs.explanation: 'Many students enrolled in many courses' is a Many-to-Many relationship — multiple records relate to multiple records in another table (typically resolved using a junction table).

mcqs.q: A Single-User Database application is one in which:
options: Only one user accesses the database at a given time
mcqs.explanation: A Single-User Database is an application where only one user accesses the database at a given time.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a classic example of a Multi-User Database application?
mcqs.explanation: An ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) application is a classic example of a multi-user database application.

mcqs.q: Which DBMS combines a DBMS and an application generator?
mcqs.explanation: Microsoft Access is a DBMS that combines a DBMS and an application generator — making it especially popular for small-business desktop databases.

mcqs.q: Which DBMS is regarded as the most difficult to use?
mcqs.explanation: Oracle (by Oracle Corporation) is regarded as the most difficult DBMS to use — because of its enterprise-scale configurability and feature depth.

mcqs.q: Oracle DBMS is produced by:
mcqs.explanation: Oracle DBMS is produced by Oracle Corporation.

mcqs.q: In an enterprise-class database system, business users typically interact with:
options: Database applications that interact with the DBMS
mcqs.explanation: In an enterprise-class database system, business users interact directly with database applications that interact with the DBMS, which then directly accesses the database data.

mcqs.q: A database design may be based on:
mcqs.explanation: A database design may be based on existing data — analysts often start by examining the data already collected by the organisation.

mcqs.q: Microsoft Access is best suited for:
options: Desktop / small-business databases combining a DBMS and app generator
mcqs.explanation: Microsoft Access is best suited for desktop and small-business databases — it combines a DBMS with a built-in application generator.

mcqs.q: SQL stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SQL stands for Structured Query Language.

mcqs.q: SQL is best described as:
options: A proprietary language used only by Oracle
An internationally recognised standard language understood by all commercial DBMS products
A markup language for the web
mcqs.explanation: SQL is an internationally recognised standard language understood by all commercial database management system products.

mcqs.q: Which language is used by every major DBMS to query and manipulate data?
mcqs.explanation: SQL (Structured Query Language) is used by every major DBMS to query and manipulate data.

mcqs.q: Which of the following BEST describes the role of SQL in modern DBMS?
options: A markup language for web pages
Standard query language understood by all commercial DBMS products
A type of file format only
mcqs.explanation: SQL is the standard query language understood by all commercial DBMS products — it is the lingua franca of databases.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is TRUE about SQL?
options: It is unique to Microsoft Access
It is internationally standardised and used by all commercial DBMS products
mcqs.explanation: SQL is internationally standardised (by ANSI & ISO) and is used by all commercial DBMS products to query and manipulate data.

mcqs.q: In which type of database is SQL most commonly used?
mcqs.explanation: SQL is most commonly used in relational databases — it is designed around the relational model.

mcqs.q: Which of the following best describes a Flat File Database?
options: Plain text file with each line typically holding one record
mcqs.explanation: A Flat File Database stores data in a plain text file with each line typically holding one record.

mcqs.q: Which database model resembles a folder/tree structure?
mcqs.explanation: The Hierarchical Database Model resembles a tree structure — similar to a folder architecture.

mcqs.q: In which database model is information represented as objects with different relationship types?
mcqs.explanation: In an Object-Oriented Database, information is represented as objects, with different types of relationships possible between two or more objects.

mcqs.q: IDMS is associated with which model?
mcqs.explanation: IDMS implemented the CODASYL DBTG model.

mcqs.q: Which technology is heavily used in database processing today for data interchange?
mcqs.explanation: The XML family of standards is very important in database processing today — used for data interchange and document storage.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a multi-user database example?
options: A single MS Access file used by one person
mcqs.explanation: An ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) application is a classic multi-user database — accessed by many users across departments simultaneously.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a single-user database example?
options: A personal MS Access file used by one person
mcqs.explanation: A personal MS Access file used by one person is a single-user database — only one user accesses it at a given time.

mcqs.q: Microsoft Access is unique because it:
options: Combines a DBMS and an application generator
mcqs.explanation: Microsoft Access combines a DBMS and an application generator — letting users build forms, reports and queries inside the same tool.

mcqs.q: Which DBMS is from Oracle Corporation?
mcqs.explanation: The Oracle DBMS is produced by Oracle Corporation — and is regarded as the most difficult DBMS to use.

mcqs.q: E. F. Codd is famous for creating which database model?
mcqs.explanation: E. F. Codd created the relational database model in 1970, defining tables, relations and the relational algebra.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the correct order historically?
mcqs.explanation: Historically: prior to 1970 data was stored in separate files (mostly on magnetic tape) → E. F. Codd's relational model in 1970 → Object-oriented databases later.

mcqs.q: In a relational database, two tables can be linked through:
mcqs.explanation: In a relational database, two tables can be linked through special key fields — typically a primary key in one and a foreign key in another.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is TRUE?
options: A flat file database has multiple related tables
A hierarchical database has tree structure
Object-oriented databases store data only as plain text
Relational databases were created by Charles Babbage
mcqs.explanation: A hierarchical database has a tree structure — similar to a folder architecture. The other statements are false.

mcqs.q: Database design can be based on:
mcqs.explanation: Database design may be based on existing data and the organisation's requirements.

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements about a database is correct?
options: A database only stores data, not its description
A database is self-describing — it stores both the data and a description of itself
Databases are not used in modern computing
A database is the same as RAM
mcqs.explanation: A database is self-describing — it stores both the data and a description of itself (its schema).

mcqs.q: Which of the following BEST describes the role of a DBMS in an enterprise-class system?
options: It is the interface between the database application and the database, controlling all data access
It is a type of hardware
It is the same as a router
mcqs.explanation: In an enterprise-class system, the DBMS acts as the interface between the database application and the database — every read/write goes through the DBMS.
